seoTitle: Insert a PDF Into Google Docs: Convert or Link It
metaDescription: Learn two ways to add a PDF to Google Docs: convert it into fully editable text via OCR, or embed it as a clickable smart chip link.
intro: PDFs aren't natively editable in Google Docs, but there are two solid workarounds. You can either convert the PDF into an editable document using Google Drive's built-in OCR, or keep the original file intact and drop in a clean, clickable link. This guide covers both paths.

steps.heading: Upload the PDF to Google Drive
steps.body: Add your target PDF file to Google Drive so it's accessible for either conversion or linking.

steps.heading: Convert with Open with Google Docs
steps.body: Right-click the PDF and choose to open it with Google Docs, which runs optical character recognition to turn it into an editable text document.

steps.heading: Review the converted text
steps.body: Check the newly generated document for accuracy, since OCR conversion can sometimes shift image placement or misalign table borders.

steps.heading: Or generate a shareable link instead
steps.body: If you'd rather keep the original PDF intact, generate a shareable view link from Drive instead of converting it.

steps.heading: Paste the link as a smart chip
steps.body: Paste the PDF's URL into your document and press Tab to convert it into an interactive smart chip that previews the file.

faq.question: Will converting a PDF to Google Docs keep the original formatting?
faq.answer: OCR conversion generally preserves most formatting, but complex layouts can shift image placement or misalign tables, so it's worth reviewing after conversion.

faq.question: What's a smart chip and why use it instead of converting?
faq.answer: A smart chip is an interactive link preview that lets readers open or view the PDF without altering the source file, which is useful when you want to keep the PDF as-is.

faq.question: Do I need special software to convert the PDF?
faq.answer: No, the conversion uses Google Drive's built-in Open with Google Docs feature, so no external software is required.

faq.question: Can I share the PDF safely once it's linked?
faq.answer: Yes, but it's worth double-checking the file's visibility and access permissions before sharing your document.
